name: hig-patterns
description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines interaction and UX patterns. Use this skill when the user asks about onboarding flow, user onboarding, app launch, loading state, drag and drop, search pattern, settings design, notifications, modality, multitasking, feedback pattern, haptics, undo redo, file management, data entry, sharing, collaboration, full screen, audio, video, haptic feedback, ratings, printing, help, or account management in Apple apps. Also use when the user says how should onboarding work, my app takes too long to load, should I use a modal here, how do I handle errors, when should I ask for permissions, how to show progress, or what's the right way to confirm a delete. Cross-references hig-foundations for underlying principles, hig-platforms for platform specifics, hig-components-layout for navigation, hig-components-content for data display.
capabilities: Provides step-by-step guidance for 25+ Apple HIG interaction patterns
Recommends appropriate UI patterns based on user goals and context
Explains platform-specific variations for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS
Identifies common pitfalls that violate Apple HIG
Covers accessibility considerations for each pattern
References official Apple documentation and best practices
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation/reference skill containing Apple HIG pattern descriptions. The scanner incorrectly flagged common documentation terms (URLs, Keychain, crypto, shell commands in code blocks) as security issues. No executable code, no network calls, no external commands - pure reference documentation.
valueStatement: Building Apple platform apps requires following Human Interface Guidelines for a consistent user experience. This skill provides expert guidance on 25+ interaction patterns including onboarding, loading, navigation, and platform-specific implementations.
limitations: Does not provide code implementations - only design guidance
Does not cover Android or Web platform patterns
Does not include visual assets or design files
Requires understanding of SwiftUI or UIKit fundamentals

useCases.title: Design onboarding flow
useCases.description: Create a brief, effective onboarding experience following Apple HIG principles with progressive disclosure and skip options

useCases.title: Implement loading states
useCases.description: Choose appropriate loading patterns including activity indicators, progress bars, and skeleton screens based on wait time and context

useCases.title: Handle destructive actions
useCases.description: Design safe destructive actions with undo support instead of excessive confirmation dialogs per Apple HIG recommendations

promptTemplates.title: Onboarding guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: How should I design onboarding for my iOS app? What are the Apple HIG recommendations for first-time user experience?

promptTemplates.title: Loading pattern selection
promptTemplates.prompt: My app takes 5+ seconds to load data. What loading pattern should I use according to Apple HIG?

promptTemplates.title: Modal usage
promptTemplates.prompt: When should I use a modal sheet vs a full-screen modal vs an alert? What does Apple HIG recommend?

promptTemplates.title: Feedback implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: How should I provide feedback to users after they complete an action? What haptics and visual indicators does Apple recommend?

bestPractices: Minimize modality - use sheets and non-blocking UI over full-screen modals whenever possible
Provide immediate feedback - every action should have visible, audible, or haptic response
Support undo over confirmation dialogs - make destructive actions reversible when feasible
antiPatterns: Splash screens with logos - show meaningful content immediately with launch screen transitioning seamlessly
Requesting all permissions at launch - request permissions contextually when needed
Excessive "Are you sure?" dialogs - prefer undo support and clear destructive button styling over confirmation fatigue

faq.question: What is the maximum number of onboarding screens recommended?
faq.answer: Apple HIG recommends a maximum of 3 onboarding screens. Keep it brief, allow users to skip, and teach through progressive disclosure.

faq.question: When should I use a modal vs a sheet?
faq.answer: Use sheets for optional tasks that can be dismissed. Use full-screen modals for focused tasks requiring completion. Use alerts for critical attention only.

faq.question: How do I handle long loading times?
faq.answer: Use skeleton screens for unknown wait times, progress bars for known durations, and activity indicators for brief waits. Never show a blank screen.

faq.question: Should I ask for sign-in immediately?
faq.answer: No, defer sign-in. Let users explore your app first. Use Sign in with Apple and support passkeys for authentication.

faq.question: How should I implement search?
faq.answer: Use search bar with suggestions, support scoped search, show recent searches, and provide results with highlighting.

faq.question: What is progressive disclosure?
faq.answer: Show essential information and controls first, then reveal advanced options as users need them. Avoid overwhelming users with every option upfront.
